data.title: SINTX Technologies Engages Veteran MedTech Executive Chris Lyons and Southern Metrics to Establish Strategic Opportunity Management Program
data.description: SINTX Technologies, Inc., a prominent figure in advanced biomaterials and silicon nitride technologies for medical and industrial use, revealed its collaboration with Southern Metrics Consulting in Salt Lake City, Utah on July 23, 2026. The aim of this engagement is to identify, assess, and pursue strategic prospects that are geared towards improving stockholder value. Southern Metrics, spearheaded by Chief Executive Officer Chris Lyons, a seasoned medical technology executive and strategic advisor who has been a part of the SINTX Board of Directors since April 2025, will take charge of establishing and supervising the Company's recently formed Strategic Opportunity Management Program. This move is expected to enhance the Company’s value proposition and bolster its position in the market.

data.title: BOS Secures $990,000 Order from a U.S. Customer
data.description: In Rishon Le Zion, Israel, on July 23, 2026, BOS Better Online Solutions Ltd. revealed that its Supply Chain Division successfully landed a purchase order worth $990,000 from a current customer based in the United States. BOS is recognized for integrating supply chain technologies across various industries including aerospace, defense, industrial, and retail sectors. The company operates under the ticker symbol BOSC on the NASDAQ stock exchange.

data.title: Kinetic Expands Fiber Footprint in Lexington by More than 20%: Community Celebration to Come
data.description: Kinetic is proud to announce the addition of over 2,800 new fiber locations in Lexington. This expansion will provide improved internet services to a larger number of residents at a competitive price, enhancing their online experience.

data.title: Farmers and Merchants Bancshares, Inc. Reports  Earnings of $3.7 Million or $1.14 Per Share For the Six Months Ended June 30, 2026
data.description: In Hampstead, Maryland, on July 23, 2026, Farmers and Merchants Bancshares, Inc. reported their financial results for the first half of the year. The parent company of Farmers and Merchants Bank revealed a net income of $3.7 million for the six months ending on June 30, 2026. This translates to $1.14 per common share (basic and diluted), showcasing a significant increase from the $2.4 million, or $0.74 per common share (basic and diluted), recorded in the same period in 2025. The Company also noted a rise in its annualized return on average assets, climbing to 0.87% for the first half of 2026 compared to 0.58% in 2025. Similarly, the annualized return on average equity saw a positive trend, reaching 11.23% for the six months ending on June 30, 2026, in contrast to 8.18% for the same period in 2025.

data.title: ACNB Corporation Reports Record 2026 Second Quarter Financial Results
data.description: ACNB Corporation, a financial holding company for ACNB Bank and ACNB Insurance Services, Inc., revealed a strong financial performance in their recent report. For the three months ended June 30, 2026, the Corporation announced a net income of $15.2 million, translating to $1.49 diluted earnings per share. This represented a notable increase from the previous quarter's net income of $13.7 million, or $1.32 diluted earnings per share for the three months ended March 31, 2026, and a substantial growth from the net income of $11.6 million, with $1.11 diluted earnings per share, for the same period in 2025. The financial outcomes for the three months ended June 30, 2025, were influenced by after-tax merger-related expenses of $1.5 million associated with the completed acquisition of Traditions Bancorp, Inc. on February 1, 2025.

data.title: Fresha Accelerates European Expansion With New Warsaw Office and Appointment of Maciej Walczewski as General Manager for Eastern Europe
data.description: In an effort to expand its presence in Poland, Fresha, the leading AI-driven booking platform for beauty and wellness services, has opened a new office in Warsaw. This move comes as part of the company's strategy to strengthen its foothold in the Eastern European region. Maciej Walczewski has been appointed as the General Manager for this market, underscoring the importance of Poland in the company's growth plans. The beauty and wellness sector in Poland is experiencing rapid growth, with a vibrant community of businesses. The number of partner businesses using Fresha's services in Poland has seen a significant increase, indicating a positive reception of the platform in this market.

data.title: Kearny Financial Corp. Announces Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year End 2026 Results and Declaration of $0.11 Per Share Cash Dividend
data.description: Kearny Financial Corp., the holding company of Kearny Bank, shared its financial results for the quarter ending June 30, 2026. The company reported a net income of $7.2 million, translating to $0.11 per diluted share, a decrease from the previous quarter's $10.1 million, or $0.16 per diluted share, as of March 31, 2026. Looking at the fiscal year ending June 30, 2026, the company disclosed a net income of $36.3 million, or $0.57 per diluted share, contrasting with the prior fiscal year's $26.1 million, or $0.42 per diluted share, ending June 30, 2025.

data.title: InterPrivate Investment Partners V Announces the Separate Trading of Its Class A Ordinary Shares and Warrants, Commencing on or About July 27, 2026
data.description: InterPrivate Investment Partners V (the “Company”) from New York disclosed that starting on or around Monday, July 27, 2026, investors holding units from the Company’s initial public offering will have the option to independently trade the Class A ordinary shares and warrants in the units.

data.title: Infrastructure Dividend Split Corp. Class A and Preferred Distributions
data.description: On July 23, 2026, Infrastructure Dividend Split Corp. (TSX: IS and IS.PR.A) declared a distribution for July 2026 to be paid to Class A shareholders. The amount of the distribution will be disclosed directly to shareholders.
